-   8d022fd: fix(mcp): support image returns in tool results

Co-authored-by: vercel-ai-sdk[bot] <225926702+vercel-ai-sdk[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
fix(provider/anthropic): parse context_management from streaming response root level (#12154)

## background

the `contextManagement` field in `providerMetadata` was always `null`
when using `streamText` with the Anthropic provider, even though the API
correctly returns `context_management` in the response.

the issue was twofold:
1. the zod schema expected `context_management` inside the `delta`
object
2. the code read from `value.delta.context_management`

but Anthropic's API returns `context_management` at the root level of
the `message_delta` event (sibling to `delta` and `usage`):

```json
{
  "type": "message_delta",
  "delta": {"stop_reason": "end_turn"},
  "usage": {...},
  "context_management": {"applied_edits": [...]}
}
```

see [Anthropic's context management
docs](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/context-management)

## summary

- moved `context_management` in the streaming schema from inside `delta`
to the root level of `message_delta`
- updated code to read from `value.context_management` instead of
`value.delta.context_management`
- added unit test for streaming context_management parsing

## verification

<details>
<summary>before fix</summary>

```
contextManagement: null
```

</details>

<details>
<summary>after fix</summary>

```
contextManagement: {
  appliedEdits: [
    {
      type: 'clear_tool_uses_20250919',
      clearedToolUses: 6,
      clearedInputTokens: 149
    }
  ]
}
```

</details>

`pnpm changeset` in root)
- [x] i have reviewed this pull request (self-review)

## related issues

fixes #12148
fix(mcp): support image returns in tool results (#12160)

## background

when an mcp server returns image content via tool results, the ai sdk's
`@ai-sdk/mcp` client stores the entire response as raw json instead of
converting it to the ai sdk's content types, causing context explosion
(200k+ tokens for large images as base64 text) and provider rejection on
subsequent requests

the mcp protocol defines image content as:
```javascript
{ type: 'image', data: 'base64...', mimeType: 'image/png' }
```

but the ai sdk expects:
```javascript
{ type: 'image-data', data: 'base64...', mediaType: 'image/png' }
```

## summary

- add `mcpToModelOutput` function to convert mcp content types to ai sdk
format
- wire `toModelOutput` property to both `dynamicTool` and `tool` calls
in mcp client
- add unit tests for content conversion (text, image, mixed, unknown
types, fallbacks)

conversions:
- `type: 'image'` + `mimeType` -> `type: 'image-data'` + `mediaType`
- `type: 'text'` -> `type: 'text'` (identity)
- unknown types -> json stringified text (fallback)

## verification

<details>
<summary>before fix (raw json storage)</summary>

```javascript
output: {
  type: 'json',
  value: {
    content: [{ type: 'image', data: 'base64...', mimeType: 'image/png' }],
    isError: false
  }
}
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>after fix (proper content type)</summary>

```javascript
output: {
  type: 'content',
  value: [{ type: 'image-data', data: 'base64...', mediaType: 'image/png' }]
}
```
</details>

## checklist

- [x] tests have been added / updated (for bug fixes / features)
- [ ] documentation has been added / updated (for bug fixes / features)
- [x] a _patch_ changeset for relevant packages has been added (run
`pnpm changeset` in root)
- [x] i have reviewed this pull request (self-review)

## future work

- handle `type: 'audio'` content when mcp spec adds it (convert to
`file-data`)
- handle embedded resource blob content (convert to `file-data` with
mimeType)

# Releases
## @ai-sdk/amazon-bedrock@4.0.41

### Patch Changes

- 0df64d6: Add `outputDimension` option for Cohere embedding models to
control the size of the output embedding vector (256, 512, 1024, or
1536).

## @ai-sdk/cohere@3.0.16

### Patch Changes

- 0df64d6: Add `outputDimension` option for Cohere embedding models to
control the size of the output embedding vector (256, 512, 1024, or
1536).

Co-authored-by: vercel-ai-sdk[bot] <225926702+vercel-ai-sdk[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
fix(amazon-bedrock/cohere): dimension option for cohere embedding models (#12127)

## Background

Cohere's Embed v4 API supports an `output_dimension` parameter that
allows controlling the size of the output embedding vector (256, 512,
1024, or 1536). This is useful for reducing storage costs and improving
performance when full-dimension embeddings aren't needed.

## Summary

**Cohere Provider (`@ai-sdk/cohere`):**
- Add `outputDimension` option to `cohereEmbeddingOptions` schema (256,
512, 1024, 1536)
- Pass `output_dimension` to the Cohere API in embedding requests
- Add test coverage for the new option
- Add example demonstrating usage with `embed-v4.0`

**Amazon Bedrock Provider (`@ai-sdk/amazon-bedrock`):**
- Add `outputDimension` option for Cohere embedding models on Bedrock
(matching the Cohere provider naming)
- Pass `output_dimension` to the Bedrock Cohere API
- Add test coverage for the new option
- Add example demonstrating usage with `cohere.embed-v4:0`

## Manual Verification

- Ran unit tests for both `@ai-sdk/cohere` and `@ai-sdk/amazon-bedrock`
packages
- Verified the Cohere example against the real Cohere API with
`embed-v4.0` model, confirming the embedding length matches the
requested dimension (256)

Co-authored-by: vercel-ai-sdk[bot] <225926702+vercel-ai-sdk[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
fix(ai): improve type validation error messages with field paths and entity identifiers (#12106)

## Background

Validation errors from `safeValidateUIMessages` and streaming validation
lacked context about what was being validated, making debugging
difficult.

**Before:**
```
Type validation failed: Value: undefined.
Error message: Required
```

**After:**
```
Type validation failed for messages[0].metadata (id: "msg-123"): Value: undefined.
Error message: Required
```

Alternative to #10154: This PR focuses on addressing the lack of context
closer to the root, and includes the full field identifier to help
finding the culprit more quickly.

## Summary

Added optional context tracking to `TypeValidationError` to include
field paths (e.g., `messages[0].parts[1].data`) and entity identifiers
(e.g., message ID, tool call ID) in validation error messages.

**Approach:**
- Introduced `TypeValidationContext` interface with `field`,
`entityName`, and `entityId` properties
- Extended `TypeValidationError` and validation utilities to accept and
format context
- Updated all validation call sites in `validate-ui-messages.ts` and
`process-ui-message-stream.ts` to provide context with index tracking

The change is backward compatible (context is optional) and includes a
minor performance improvement by consolidating data/tool validation into
a single loop.

## Manual Verification

To verify the improved error messages:

```typescript
import { validateUIMessages } from 'ai';
import { z } from 'zod';

try {
  await validateUIMessages({
    messages: [{
      id: 'msg-123',
      role: 'user',
      metadata: { userId: 123 }, // wrong type
      parts: [{ type: 'text', text: 'Hello' }],
    }],
    metadataSchema: z.object({ userId: z.string() }),
  });
} catch (error) {
  console.log(error.message);
  // Shows: "Type validation failed for messages[0].metadata (id: "msg-123"): ..."
}
```

## Checklist

- [ ] Documentation has been added / updated (for bug fixes / features)

fixes / features - run `pnpm changeset` in the project root)
- [x] I have reviewed this pull request (self-review)

Issue #10137 also mentions fixing documentation for metadata schema
examples. That's not the primary issue raised though, and will be
addressed in a separate PR.

feat(github-action): rename 'examples/ai-functions' to 'examples/ai-core' when backporting (#12078)

## Background

due to the recent directory rename of the `example/ai-core`, when a PR
with an example under `example/ai-functions` is merged and backported,
the directory isn't mapped back to the release-v5.0 equivalen.

which leads to release actions breaking as seen in
https://github.com/vercel/ai/actions/runs/21389777800/job/61573678670

## Summary

added a new step where we rename any examples under
`example/ai-functions` to `example/ai-core` and add that commit

feat(ai): add experimental retention setting (#12126)

## Background

The request and response bodies from the requests are kept in the step
information, even though they are rarely used. This leads to high memory
consumption, especially in cases where images are used.

## Summary

Add `experimental_retention` parameter to enable excluding request
bodies and response bodies. You can opt in to reduce memory consumption.

## Example

```ts
const result = await generateText({
  model,
  messages,
  // Disable retention of request body to reduce memory usage
  experimental_retention: {
    requestBody: false,
    responseBody: false,
  },
});
```

## Future Work

* move `includeRawChunks` into `retention` as `rawChunks`
* disable retention by default (breaking change, AI SDK 7) to lower
memory usage

## Related Issues

Fixes #10882

yet, that's fine, whenever you add more changesets to main, this PR will
be updated.

docs: fix incorrect and outdated provider docs (#12094)

## Background
The AI SDK documentation contains incorrect and outdated information.
This impacts the performance of coding agents.

## Summary
AI code review and fixes to improve provider documentation correctness
(except for openai, anthropic, xai, google)

## Related Issues
Continuation of #12071
fix(ci): use github api for changed files in triage workflow (#12113)

## background

triage workflow fails for PRs targeting `release-v5.0` because shallow
checkout doesn't have enough history to find merge base:
```
fatal: origin/release-v5.0...HEAD: no merge base
```

also, output variable was `changed_files` but prompt referenced
`outputs.files`

## summary

- add `fetch-depth: 0` to checkout step to fetch full history
- fix output variable name from `changed_files` to `files`
- remove redundant `git fetch` command

## Background

AI Gateway is adding support for the parallel.ai search tool.

## Summary

Added a new Parallel Search tool to the AI SDK Gateway provider that enables models to search the web using Parallel AI's Search API. This tool is optimized for LLM consumption and supports different search types for depth vs breadth tradeoffs.

## Manual Verification

# Releases
## @ai-sdk/xai@3.0.40

### Patch Changes

-   05f3f36: Add native `file_search` server-side tool support:

- Add `xai.tools.fileSearch()` for vector store search with
`vectorStoreIds` and `maxNumResults` parameters
- Add `include` option supporting `file_search_call.results` to get
inline search results
- Add `file_search_call` handling in language model for both
`doGenerate` and `doStream`
- Fix invalid `grok-4-1` model type (only `grok-4-1-fast-reasoning` and
`grok-4-1-fast-non-reasoning` exist)
- Fix `toolChoice` for server-side tools: xAI API doesn't support
forcing specific server-side tools, now emits warning instead of sending
invalid request

Co-authored-by: vercel-ai-sdk[bot] <225926702+vercel-ai-sdk[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Feat(xai): file search tool & fixes (#12040)

## Background

The xAI Responses API supports a `file_search` server-side tool for
searching vector stores (collections), but it was not implemented in the
SDK.

Additionally, the SDK was generating invalid `toolChoice` formats (e.g.,
`{ type: 'web_search' }`) for server-side tools, which xAI's API doesn't
support - only `"auto"`, `"none"`, `"required"`, and `{"type":
"function", "function": {"name": "..."}}` are valid.

## Summary

- Add native `file_search` server-side tool support via
`xai.tools.fileSearch()`
- Add `include` option supporting `file_search_call.results` to get
inline search results
- Add `file_search_call` output item type and streaming events
(`in_progress`, `searching`, `completed`)
- Fix `toolChoice` for server-side tools: now emits warning instead of
sending invalid API request
- Fix invalid `grok-4-1` model type (only `grok-4-1-fast-reasoning` and
`grok-4-1-fast-non-reasoning` exist)
- Update xAI provider documentation with file_search examples

## Manual Verification

Tested with real xAI API using `grok-4-1-fast-reasoning` model:
- ✅ `generateText` with file_search tool returns results correctly
- ✅ `streamText` with file_search tool streams text with tool calls
- ✅ Streaming events (`in_progress`, `searching`, `completed`) parsed
without validation errors
- ✅ `include: ['file_search_call.results']` returns inline search
results
- ✅ Verified `toolChoice` for server-side tools fails with xAI API
(tested `web_search`, `code_interpreter`, `x_search`, `file_search`)

## Checklist

- [x] Tests have been added / updated (for bug fixes / features)
- [x] Documentation has been added / updated (for bug fixes / features)
- [x] A _patch_ changeset for relevant packages has been added (for bug
fixes / features - run `pnpm changeset` in the project root)
- [x] I have reviewed this pull request (self-review)

## Future Work

- Add metadata filters support:
https://docs.x.ai/docs/guides/using-collections/metadata

### Patch Changes

- 87e5e2a: Fix typo in README.md: `devToolsMiddleware` should be called
as a function `devToolsMiddleware()`.

Co-authored-by: vercel-ai-sdk[bot] <225926702+vercel-ai-sdk[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
docs(devtools): correct middleware function call in README example (#12090)

## Background

The README example shows incorrect usage of `devToolsMiddleware`. It was
passed as a reference instead of being called as a factory function,
which would cause runtime errors.

## Summary

- Fixed the middleware usage example in `packages/devtools/README.md`
- Changed `middleware: devToolsMiddleware` to `middleware:
devToolsMiddleware()`

The `devToolsMiddleware` is a factory function that returns a
`LanguageModelV3Middleware` instance, so it needs to be invoked with
`()`.

## Summary

#28](https://github.com/BITASIA/sap-ai-provider/pull/28) -
LanguageModelV3 migration). The new package is actively maintained with
regular releases.

`@jerome-benoit/sap-ai-provider`
- **Dual package support**: Documents both V3 (AI SDK 5.x/6.x) and V2
(AI SDK 5.x) providers
- **Documentation rewrite**: Harmonized with Vercel AI SDK conventions

- Fix undefined assistant message content in tool-only responses
- Fix `topK` and `seed` parameter handling
- Fix nested `modelParams` propagation (deep merge)
- Fix orchestration modules missing in streaming requests
- Fix Jinja2 delimiter escaping conflicts with orchestration templates

## New Features

- **Language Model V3** compliance (AI SDK 6.x)
- **Text embeddings** support via `.embeddingModel()`
- **595 tests** with 92%+ coverage
- **Template escaping** for SAP orchestration pipelines

# Releases
## @ai-sdk/xai@3.0.39

### Patch Changes

- 58800f3: fix(xai): emit reasoning-start before reasoning-end for
encrypted reasoning

Co-authored-by: vercel-ai-sdk[bot] <225926702+vercel-ai-sdk[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
fix(xai): emit reasoning-start before reasoning-end for encrypted reasoning (#12086)

## Summary

Fixes "reasoning part not found" errors when streaming responses from
xAI models with encrypted reasoning (e.g., `grok-4-fast-reasoning`,
`grok-4.1-fast-reasoning`).

## Problem

When xAI returns encrypted reasoning content, the
`response.output_item.done` event may arrive without a prior
`response.reasoning_summary_part.added` event. This caused
`reasoning-end` to be emitted without a preceding `reasoning-start`,
which the AI SDK's stream processing logic cannot handle.

The AI SDK tracks active reasoning parts and expects `reasoning-start`
before `reasoning-end`:
```
reasoning part reasoning-rs_XXX not found
```

## Solution

Track which reasoning blocks have had `reasoning-start` emitted. Before
emitting `reasoning-end`, check if `reasoning-start` was already emitted
for that block. If not, emit `reasoning-start` first.

## Test Plan

- Run existing xai package tests: `pnpm test --filter @ai-sdk/xai`
- Test streaming with `xai/grok-4-fast-reasoning` model to verify no
"reasoning part not found" errors

Fix handling of error results in deferrable tools (#12011)

## Background

Right now if a deferrable tool (like Anthropic's web fetch) has an
error, the error is not treated as a result, so the AI SDK calls
Anthropic again immediately.

## Summary

Remove tools from pendingDeferredToolCalls when an error occurs

## Manual Verification

Tested this fix manually against my app where I'm having this issue.

populate the `text` field. anthropic was the only provider with this bug

instead of `text: undefined`

tests
- add example `anthropic-usage-tokens.ts` to verify fix
- update 29 snapshots that now correctly show `text` populated

## verification

1. reverted fix, ran tests - 34 tests failed showing `text: undefined`
2. reverted fix, ran example - output showed "BUG: textTokens is
undefined but should be 5"
3. applied fix, ran tests - all 260 tests pass
4. applied fix, ran example - output showed "OK: textTokens is correctly
populated" with `textTokens: 5`

# Releases
## @ai-sdk/amazon-bedrock@4.0.35

### Patch Changes

-   Updated dependencies [572ea12]
    -   @ai-sdk/anthropic@3.0.27

## @ai-sdk/anthropic@3.0.27

### Patch Changes

- 572ea12: feat(anthropic): allow custom/dynamic key for providerOptions

## @ai-sdk/google-vertex@4.0.32

### Patch Changes

-   Updated dependencies [572ea12]
    -   @ai-sdk/anthropic@3.0.27

Co-authored-by: vercel-ai-sdk[bot] <225926702+vercel-ai-sdk[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
fix(anthropic): allow custom/dynamic key for providerOptions (#11998)

## Background

#11951

users couldn't pass options using the custom name for their provider

## Summary

- parse providerOptions from both canonical and custom keys
- conditionally include custom key in providerMetadata output

example 1:

```ts
providerOptions: {
  anthropic: {
    user: 'test-user-canonical',
  },
},

print(result.providerMetadata);
// will only have the 'anthropic' key
```

example 2:

```ts
providerOptions: {
  'my-custom-openai': {
    user: 'test-user-canonical',
  },
},

print(result.providerMetadata);
// will have the 'anthropic' key AND 'my-custom-openai' key (with the exact same values
```

PS: unchanged behaviour for default providers

## Manual Verification

- verified for multi-turn conversations by changing the agent for
`http://localhost:3000/chat-openai-smooth-stream` to be implemented via
custom provider with a custom providerOptions key
- added cli examples to test

chore: fix `pnpm dev` error and warning (#12045)

## Background

Tried running `pnpm dev` and it led to a warning and an error:
```
WARNING  --no-cache is deprecated and will be removed in a future major version. Use --cache=local:r,remote:r
turbo 2.4.4

  × Invalid task configuration
  ╰─▶   × You have 20 persistent tasks but `turbo` is configured for concurrency of 16. Set --concurrency to at least 21

 ELIFECYCLE  Command failed with exit code 1.
```

## Summary

* used the non-deprecated way to express "no cache"
* set concurrency to 25, to be above 20, but with a little headroom

## Manual Verification

Command successfully executes with these fixes, and without warning.

chore: standardize jsdoc comment format (#12068)

## Background

We have 2 ways of formatting jsdoc comments in our repository: indented
with `*` and without indentation /`*`. AI agents by default gravitate to
towards the first style.

## Summary

fix(provider-utils): recognize bun fetch errors as retryable (#12048)

## Background

network errors during `fetch()` are not retried when running on Bun
because `handleFetchError` only recognizes Node.js/browser error
patterns. Bun throws different error types with different codes.

## Summary

- add detection for Bun's network error codes (`ConnectionRefused`,
`ConnectionClosed`, `FailedToOpenSocket`, `ECONNRESET`, etc.)
- add `isBunNetworkError()` helper function
- mark Bun network errors as retryable, matching Node.js/browser
behavior

## Verification

<details>
<summary>test results</summary>

**before fix:** 4 Bun tests fail
```
FAIL  src/handle-fetch-error.test.ts > handleFetchError > bun fetch errors > should handle ConnectionRefused error
FAIL  src/handle-fetch-error.test.ts > handleFetchError > bun fetch errors > should handle ConnectionClosed error
FAIL  src/handle-fetch-error.test.ts > handleFetchError > bun fetch errors > should handle FailedToOpenSocket error
FAIL  src/handle-fetch-error.test.ts > handleFetchError > bun fetch errors > should handle ECONNRESET error
```

**after fix:** all 393 tests pass
```
Test Files  49 passed (49)
Tests       393 passed (393)
```

</details>

Co-authored-by: vercel-ai-sdk[bot] <225926702+vercel-ai-sdk[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
fix(ai): clean up step timeout when error occurs in streamText (#12047)

## Background

when `streamText` is called with `timeout.stepMs`, a `setTimeout` is
created at the start of each step. if the model's `doStream` throws an
error before the stream is processed, the timeout is never cleared,
causing the node.js process to hang until the timer expires.

## Summary

- wrap step execution in `try/finally` to ensure `clearStepTimeout()`
and `clearChunkTimeout()` are called even when `doStream` throws
- matches the existing pattern used in `generate-text.ts`

## Verification

<details>
<summary>test script</summary>

```typescript
import { MockLanguageModelV3 } from 'ai/test';
import { streamText } from 'ai';

async function main() {
  console.log('Starting test at', new Date().toISOString());

  try {
    const result = streamText({
      model: new MockLanguageModelV3({
        doStream: async () => {
          throw new Error('Fail immediately');
        },
      }),
      prompt: 'test-input',
      maxRetries: 0,
      timeout: { stepMs: 5000 },
      onError: () => {},
    });

    await result.consumeStream();
  } catch (e) {
    // ignore
  }

  console.log('Stream consumed at', new Date().toISOString());
  console.log('If timeout leaks, process will hang for ~5 seconds...');
}

main();
```

</details>

**before fix:** process hangs for 5 seconds after stream is consumed
**after fix:** process exits immediately

## Checklist

- [x] tests have been added / updated (for bug fixes / features)
- [ ] documentation has been added / updated (for bug fixes / features)
- [x] a _patch_ changeset for relevant packages has been added (run

# Releases
## @ai-sdk/amazon-bedrock@4.0.31

### Patch Changes

- 151c4aa: Add support for Amazon Nova embedding models by sending the
required `taskType` and `singleEmbeddingParams` payload and parsing
Nova-style responses.

Co-authored-by: vercel-ai-sdk[bot] <225926702+vercel-ai-sdk[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
fix(amazon-bedrock): support Nova embedding models (#11354)

## Background

Nova embedding models on Amazon Bedrock use a different API schema than
Titan models. Nova requires a `SINGLE_EMBEDDING` taskType with
`singleEmbeddingParams`, while Titan uses `inputText`. The response
formats also differ: Nova returns `embeddings` (array of objects), Titan
returns `embedding` (array of numbers).

## Summary

- Update `BedrockEmbeddingModel.doEmbed` to send a `SINGLE_EMBEDDING`
payload for Nova embedding models with `singleEmbeddingParams`
containing `embeddingPurpose`, `embeddingDimension`, and `text` with
`truncationMode`.
- Add Nova-specific provider options: `embeddingDimension` (256, 384,
1024, 3072), `embeddingPurpose` ('GENERIC_INDEX', 'QUERY')
- Update response schema to handle both Nova (`embeddings` array) and
Titan (`embedding`) response formats using a union schema.
- Add/extend tests to assert the Nova embeddings request and response
schema.

## Manual Verification

- Ran the updated unit tests covering Nova embeddings request schema
(`packages/amazon-bedrock/src/bedrock-embedding-model.test.ts`).
- Verified against a real Bedrock Nova embeddings model by calling
`doEmbed` and confirming the request payload uses `SINGLE_EMBEDDING`
taskType.

- [ ] Documentation has been added / updated (for bug fixes / features)

fixes / features - run `pnpm changeset` in the project root)
- [x] I have reviewed this pull request (self-review)

## @ai-sdk/openai@3.0.19

### Patch Changes

- 04c89b1: Provide Responses API providerMetadata types at the message /
reasoning level.

    -   Export the following types for use in client code:
        -   `OpenaiResponsesProviderMetadata`
        -   `OpenaiResponsesReasoningProviderMetadata`
        -   `AzureResponsesProviderMetadata`
        -   `AzureResponsesReasoningProviderMetadata`

Co-authored-by: vercel-ai-sdk[bot] <225926702+vercel-ai-sdk[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
feat,docs(openai,azure):provide Responses API providerMetadata types at the provider-specific metadata / reasoning part metadata (#12010)

## Background

The Responses API attaches provider-specific metadata to messages and
reasoning parts, but until now there were no exported, typed helpers for
accessing this metadata in a type-safe way from client code.

As a result, users had to rely on untyped or loosely typed access to
`providerMetadata`, especially when working with response IDs, logprobs,
service tier information, or reasoning-related metadata.

## Summary

This PR adds and documents typed providerMetadata helpers for the
Responses API at the message and reasoning level.

- Export new providerMetadata types for both OpenAI and Azure:
  - `OpenaiResponsesProviderMetadata`
  - `OpenaiResponsesReasoningProviderMetadata`
  - `AzureResponsesProviderMetadata`
  - `AzureResponsesReasoningProviderMetadata`
- Ensure providerMetadata construction is type-checked internally using
`satisfies`.
- Update OpenAI and Azure provider documentation with:
  - Typed examples for accessing message-level providerMetadata.
  - New sections describing typed providerMetadata in reasoning parts.
- Update examples to demonstrate practical usage of the new types
(e.g. `responseId`, `logprobs`, `serviceTier`,
`reasoningEncryptedContent`).

No behavior changes are introduced; this change improves type safety,
documentation, and developer ergonomics.

## Manual Verification

### examples in `examples/ai-functions`
Edited for add types and run tests.
- pnpm tsx src/generate-text/azure-reasoning-encrypted-content.ts
- pnpm tsx src/generate-text/azure-reasoning.ts
- pnpm tsx src/generate-text/openai-logprobs.ts

- pnpm tsx src/generate-text/openai-reasoning.ts
- pnpm tsx src/generate-text/openai-responses-previous-response-id.ts
- pnpm tsx src/stream-text/azure-fullstream-logprobs.ts
- pnpm tsx src/stream-text/azure-reasoning-encrypted-content.ts
- pnpm tsx src/stream-text/azure-reasoning.ts
- pnpm tsx src/stream-text/openai-fullstream-logprobs.ts

# Releases
## @ai-sdk/xai@3.0.34

### Patch Changes

- 648c8f3: fix(xai): make usage nullable in responses schema for
streaming compatibility

xAI sends `usage: null` in early streaming events (`response.created`,
`response.in_progress`) because token counts aren't available until the
stream completes. This change makes the `usage` field nullish in
`xaiResponsesResponseSchema` to accept these values without validation
errors.

Co-authored-by: vercel-ai-sdk[bot] <225926702+vercel-ai-sdk[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
fix(xai): make usage nullable in responses schema for streaming compatibility (#12004)

<!--
Welcome to contributing to AI SDK! We're excited to see your changes.

We suggest you read the following contributing guide we've created
before submitting:

https://github.com/vercel/ai/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
-->

## Background

xAI returns `usage: null` in early streaming response events
(`response.created`, `response.in_progress`) because token counts aren't
available until the stream completes. The actual usage is sent in the
final `response.completed` event.

This causes `AI_TypeValidationError` during streaming because the schema
expects `usage` to be an object, not null.

## Summary

Make `usage` field nullish in `xaiResponsesResponseSchema` to accept
null values during streaming

## Manual Verification

1. Run a streaming request to xAI via the gateway:
```ts
  import { streamText } from 'ai';
  import { createGateway } from '@ai-sdk/gateway';

  const gateway = createGateway({ baseURL: '...', apiKey: '...' });
  const result = streamText({
    model: gateway('xai/grok-3-fast'),
    prompt: 'Say hello',
  });

  for await (const part of result.fullStream) {
    console.log(part.type);
  }
  console.log('Usage:', await result.usage);
```
2. Verify no AI_TypeValidationError is logged for response.created /
response.in_progress events
3. Verify final usage is correctly captured

# Releases
## @ai-sdk/amazon-bedrock@4.0.30

### Patch Changes

- 6ece44c: Fix Cohere embedding model request format on Bedrock by
sending the required `input_type` and parsing Cohere-style responses.

Co-authored-by: vercel-ai-sdk[bot] <225926702+vercel-ai-sdk[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
fix(amazon-bedrock): support Cohere embedding models (#11213)

## Background

Cohere embedding model IDs (e.g. `cohere.embed-english-v3`,
`cohere.embed-multilingual-v3`) are listed as supported by
`@ai-sdk/amazon-bedrock`, but embedding requests fail at runtime because
Cohere-on-Bedrock requires a different request schema than Titan
(notably a required `input_type`). This prevents Cohere embedding models
from being usable through the Bedrock provider and triggers a 400
“Malformed input request” error. Fixes
[#5055](https://github.com/vercel/ai/issues/5055).

## Summary

- Updated `BedrockEmbeddingModel` to detect `cohere.embed-*` model IDs
and:
- Send Cohere-compatible request payload including required `input_type`
(defaulting to `search_query`) and optional `truncate`.
- Parse Cohere-style embedding responses (`{ embeddings: number[][] }`)
in addition to Titan-style responses.
- Extended `bedrockEmbeddingProviderOptions` with Cohere-specific
options:
- `inputType` (`search_document | search_query | classification |
clustering`)
  - `truncate` (`NONE | START | END`)
- Added/updated unit tests for Cohere embedding request/response
handling.
- Updated Bedrock provider docs to mention the required Cohere
`inputType`.
- Added a **patch changeset** for `@ai-sdk/amazon-bedrock`.

## Manual Verification

- Verified end-to-end by running `embed()` against Amazon Bedrock using
`cohere.embed-english-v3` and confirming the API call succeeds and
returns a numeric embedding vector (previously returned a 400 validation
error complaining about missing `input_type`).

## Checklist

- [x] Tests have been added / updated (for bug fixes / features)
- [x] Documentation has been added / updated (for bug fixes / features)
- [x] A _patch_ changeset for relevant packages has been added (for bug
fixes / features - run `pnpm changeset` in the project root)
- [x] I have reviewed this pull request (self-review)

## Future Work

- If/when Bedrock exposes additional Cohere embedding model IDs (e.g.
newer versions), we can consider documenting them explicitly, but the
provider already supports arbitrary model IDs via `string` model IDs.

## Related Issues

Fixes
- [#5055](https://github.com/vercel/ai/issues/5055)
- [#9390](https://github.com/vercel/ai/issues/9390)

Co-authored-by: vercel-ai-sdk[bot] <225926702+vercel-ai-sdk[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
chore: excluded tests from src folder in npm package (#11958)

## Background

The `src` folders in the npm packages, which are published primarily as
grep targets for coding agents, include tests and related files. This
increases package size and makes searching by coding agents less
efficient.

## Summary

Exclude tests files, test fixtures, test snapshots from `src` in npm
packages.

## Manual Verification

- [x] verify that snapshot does not include test files

## Related Issues

Sources in npm packages were introduced in #11950 and #11790
Version Packages (#11963)

you're ready to do a release, you can merge this and the packages will
be published to npm automatically. If you're not ready to do a release
yet, that's fine, whenever you add more changesets to main, this PR will
be updated.

## @ai-sdk/anthropic@3.0.22

### Patch Changes

- 8ccf04b: Add `authToken` option to support `Authorization: Bearer`
authentication as an alternative to `x-api-key` header authentication.

## @ai-sdk/google-vertex@4.0.27

### Patch Changes

-   Updated dependencies [8ccf04b]
    -   @ai-sdk/anthropic@3.0.22

Co-authored-by: vercel-ai-sdk[bot] <225926702+vercel-ai-sdk[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
feat(anthropic): add authToken option for Bearer authentication (#11782)

## Background

Some use cases require `Authorization: Bearer` header authentication
instead of the `x-api-key` header when using the Anthropic API. This is
particularly useful when working with proxy services or custom API
gateways that expect Bearer token authentication.

## Summary

Added a new `authToken` option to the Anthropic provider that enables
`Authorization: Bearer` header authentication as an alternative to the
existing `x-api-key` authentication method.

Changes:
- Added `authToken` option to `AnthropicProviderSettings`
- Added support for `ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN` environment variable
- When `authToken` is provided, uses `Authorization: Bearer` header
instead of `x-api-key`

## Manual Verification

Tested locally by configuring the provider with `authToken` option and
verifying the correct `Authorization: Bearer` header is sent in API
requests.

# Releases
## @ai-sdk/xai@3.0.32

### Patch Changes

-   27d0c05: feat(xai): add mcpServer tool for remote MCP support

Co-authored-by: vercel-ai-sdk[bot] <225926702+vercel-ai-sdk[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
feat(xai): add mcpServer tool for remote MCP support (#11781)

## Background

xAI's Responses API supports [remote MCP (Model Context Protocol)
servers](https://docs.x.ai/docs/guides/responses-api#mcp-tools) as a
tool type, allowing models to connect to external MCP servers and use
their tools. This capability is currently not exposed in the AI SDK's
xAI provider.

## Summary

Adds `xai.tools.mcpServer()` for connecting to remote MCP servers via
xAI's Responses API.

**New exports:**
- `xai.tools.mcpServer({ serverUrl, serverLabel, ... })` -
provider-defined tool factory

**Changes:**
- Added `mcp-server.ts` tool factory with full configuration options
(serverUrl, serverLabel, serverDescription, allowedTools, headers,
authorization)
- Updated `xai-responses-api.ts` with MCP tool type definition and
streaming event schemas (`response.mcp_call.*` events)
- Updated `xai-responses-language-model.ts` to handle `mcp_call` output
items in both streaming and non-streaming modes
- Updated `xai-responses-prepare-tools.ts` to transform MCP tool args
(camelCase → snake_case)
- Exported `mcpServer` from `packages/xai/src/tool/index.ts`

**Usage:**
```typescript
import { xai } from '@ai-sdk/xai';
import { streamText } from 'ai';

const result = await streamText({
  model: xai('grok-3'),
  tools: {
    myMcpServer: xai.tools.mcpServer({
      serverUrl: 'https://example.com/mcp',
      serverLabel: 'my-server',
      serverDescription: 'My remote MCP server',
      allowedTools: ['tool1', 'tool2'],
      authorization: 'Bearer token',
    }),
  },
  prompt: 'Use the MCP server to...',
});
```

## Manual Verification

Tested in a Next.js application with xAI's Responses API:
1. Created MCP server tool with various configurations
2. Verified tool preparation transforms args correctly (camelCase →
snake_case)
3. Verified streaming events are handled (`mcp_call` output items,
`response.mcp_call.*` events)
4. Verified non-streaming mode handles `mcp_call` output correctly

## Checklist

- [x] Tests have been added / updated (for bug fixes / features)
- [x] Documentation has been added / updated (for bug fixes / features)
- [x] A _patch_ changeset for relevant packages has been added (for bug
fixes / features - run `pnpm changeset` in the project root)
- [x] I have reviewed this pull request (self-review)

## Future Work

- Add documentation page for MCP tool in xAI provider docs
- Consider adding MCP-specific streaming events to the SDK's stream
parts (e.g., `mcp-call-start`, `mcp-call-complete`)

## Related Issues

None - this is a new feature request.

Co-authored-by: Aayush Kapoor <83492835+aayush-kapoor@users.noreply.github.com>

## @ai-sdk/google-vertex@4.0.26

### Patch Changes

-   Updated dependencies [662d359]
    -   @ai-sdk/anthropic@3.0.21

Co-authored-by: vercel-ai-sdk[bot] <225926702+vercel-ai-sdk[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
feat(anthropic): deferred results for tool search tool (#11931)

## Background

anthropic sometimes sends the tool results for a
`tool_search_tool_regex/bm25` tool call in the next pass. this leads to
the SDK's assumptions breaking and thus leads to errors seen in #11849

## Summary

- add the supportDeferredResults param for both tool search tools
- proper name mapping of the tools when they come back in the next pass
of response (was being defaulted to regex tool for both) when missing
the OG tool call block

## Manual Verification

verified via running the example
`examples/ai-functions/src/generate-text/anthropic-tool-search-deferred-bm25.ts`
before and after + added unit tests that simulate the behaviour

## @ai-sdk/openai@3.0.17

### Patch Changes

-   4218f86: fix(openai): preserve tool id for apply patch tool

Co-authored-by: vercel-ai-sdk[bot] <225926702+vercel-ai-sdk[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
fix(openai): preserve tool id for apply patch tool (#11938)

## Background

when using `store: false` with openai's apply_patch tool in multi-step
conversations, the SDK was incorrectly converting tool calls back to the
API format -> converted these tool calls as `function_call` messages

## Summary

added special handling for `apply_patch` tool calls, simialr to what we
do for `shell` tool handling

## Manual Verification

verified by adding `store: false` providerOption to the example
`examples/ai-functions/src/generate-text/openai-responses-apply-patch.ts`,
which failed before the fix and worked after.

feat(gateway): add generationId to gateway errors (#11798)

## Background

When gateway errors occur, the response often includes a `generationId`
that can be used for debugging and tracing issues with the AI Gateway
team. Currently, this ID is not exposed to users, making it harder to
report and investigate errors.

  ## Summary

  Added `generationId` support to all gateway error classes:

  - Added `generationId` property to the base `GatewayError` class
- Included `generationId` in error messages (e.g., `"Rate limit exceeded
[gen_01ABC123]"`) for visibility in stack traces
- Updated all error subclasses (`GatewayAuthenticationError`,
`GatewayInternalServerError`, `GatewayInvalidRequestError`,
`GatewayModelNotFoundError`, `GatewayRateLimitError`,
`GatewayResponseError`) to accept and propagate `generationId`
- Updated `createGatewayErrorFromResponse` to extract `generationId`
from error responses
  - Updated the response schema to parse the `generationId` field

  ## Manual Verification

Verified that errors thrown from the gateway include the `generationId`
both in the error message and as an accessible property on the error
object.

  ## Checklist

  - [x] Tests have been added / updated (for bug fixes / features)
- [ ] Documentation has been added / updated (for bug fixes / features)
- [x] A _patch_ changeset for relevant packages has been added (for bug
fixes / features - run `pnpm changeset` in the project root)
  - [x] I have reviewed this pull request (self-review)

# Releases
## @ai-sdk/amazon-bedrock@4.0.24

### Patch Changes

- d5466df: fix(amazon-bedrock): normalize tool call IDs for Mistral
models

Mistral models on Bedrock require tool call IDs to match exactly 9
alphanumeric characters. This fix normalizes Bedrock-generated tool call
IDs when using Mistral models to prevent validation errors during
multi-turn tool calling.

Co-authored-by: vercel-ai-sdk[bot] <225926702+vercel-ai-sdk[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
fix(amazon-bedrock): normalize tool call IDs for Mistral models (#11863)

## Background

Mistral models on Bedrock require tool call IDs to match exactly 9
alphanumeric characters (regex `^[a-zA-Z0-9]{9}$`). However, Bedrock
generates tool call IDs in formats like `tooluse_bpe71yCfRu2b5i-nKGDr5g`
which are incompatible with Mistral validation. This causes tool calling
to fail on the second turn when sending the tool result back.

## Summary

- Added `isMistralModel()` helper to detect Mistral models (prefixed
with `mistral.` or region-prefixed like `us.mistral.`)
- Added `normalizeToolCallId()` to extract first 9 alphanumeric
characters from IDs
- Applied normalization when receiving tool calls from Mistral models in
`doGenerate` and `doStream`
- Applied normalization when sending tool results to Mistral models in
`convertToBedrockChatMessages`
- Maintained original behavior for non-Mistral models

## Checklist

- [x] Tests have been added / updated (for bug fixes / features)
- [ ] Documentation has been added / updated (for bug fixes / features)
- [x] A _patch_ changeset for relevant packages has been added (for bug
fixes / features - run `pnpm changeset` in the project root)
- [x] I have reviewed this pull request (self-review)

docs(providers): add Apertis community provider (#11889)

## Summary

Add Apertis as a community provider for the AI SDK.

[Apertis](https://apertis.ai) is a unified AI gateway providing access
to 470+ models from leading providers including OpenAI, Anthropic,
Google, and more through a single API.

## Features

- Chat models via `apertis()` or `apertis.chat()`
- Embedding models via `apertis.textEmbeddingModel()`
- Support for 470+ models including GPT-5.2, Claude Opus 4.5, Gemini 3,
and more

- npm:
[@apertis/ai-sdk-provider](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@apertis/ai-sdk-provider)

Co-authored-by: vercel-ai-sdk[bot] <225926702+vercel-ai-sdk[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
fix(anthropic): process inline citations from webSearch and webFetch tools (#11862)

## Background

Fixes #11835. When using webSearch or webFetch tools with citations
enabled, inline citations (citations_delta events with
web_search_result_location type) were being silently dropped. The SDK's
createCitationSource() only handled page_location and char_location
types.

## Summary

Added handling for `web_search_result_location` citations and fixed
webFetch document tracking:

- Extended `createCitationSource()` to handle
`web_search_result_location` type
- Added webFetch documents to `citationDocuments[]` so `char_location`
citations can resolve metadata

## Manual Verification

**webSearch:**
modified
examples/ai-functions/src/stream-text/anthropic-web-search-tool.ts to
log full source objects (instead of just URL sources). Console output
showed sources containing citedText and encryptedIndex in
providerMetadata.anthropic, confirming web_search_result_location
citations are now handled.

**webFetch:**
i temporarily modified
examples/ai-functions/src/stream-text/anthropic-web-fetch-tool-wikipedia.ts
to enable citations: { enabled: true } and log full source objects. Ran
the example and verified source events appeared with citedText,
startCharIndex, and endCharIndex in providerMetadata.anthropic,
confirming char_location citations now resolve document metadata
correctly.
```{
  "type": "source",
  "sourceType": "document",
  "id": "U2b7e0aQhoQlx6Oc",
  "mediaType": "text/plain",
  "title": "Maglemosian culture",
  "providerMetadata": {
    "anthropic": {
      "citedText": "Maglemosian (c. 9000 – c. 6000 BC) is the name given to a culture...",
      "startCharIndex": 47,
      "endCharIndex": 331
    }
  }
}
```
i also tested using code from issue #11835. Before the fix, the stream
showed fragmented text-start/text-end events at citation boundaries but
no citation data (as reported in the issue). After the fix, source
events now appear between text blocks. eg:

```
{"type":"text-delta","id":"7","text":"\n\n"}
{"type":"text-end","id":"7"}
{"type":"text-start","id":"8"}
{"type":"source","sourceType":"document","id":"9MdpFtow2DjgV7Zh","mediaType":"text/plain","title":"Tool search tool","providerMeta....(too long)
.
.
. and invokes them\n\nThis keeps your context window efficient while maintaining high tool selection accuracy.\n\n","startCharIndex":11996,"endCharIndex":12987}}}
{"type":"text-delta","id":"8","text":"There"}
{"type":"text-delta","id":"8","text":" are two tool"}
```

## Checklist

- [ ] Documentation has been added / updated (for bug fixes / features)

## @ai-sdk/google-vertex@4.0.22

### Patch Changes

- 689557c: set Google Vertex Express API key as header instead of URL
argument

Co-authored-by: vercel-ai-sdk[bot] <225926702+vercel-ai-sdk[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
fix(google-vertex): pass express API key as header instead of URL search parameter (#11919)

## Background

The original PR (https://github.com/vercel/ai/pull/10931) stated
"requires the api key to be added as a search parameter" but on this
page it says that it can be passed as header as well:
https://cloud.google.com/resources/cloud-express-faqs?hl=en

That is preferable since URLs get logged and that can leak API keys

## Summary

Replace query search parameter with header

    -   ai@6.0.43

Co-authored-by: vercel-ai-sdk[bot] <225926702+vercel-ai-sdk[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
fix(ai): handle provider-executed tools and tool-approval-response in validation (#11778)

## Background

This PR addresses issue #11776 where a "Message Validation Error"
occurred after a tool call was approved. The fix has been expanded to
handle two scenarios:

1. **Provider-executed tools** (like Anthropic's `code_execution`) that
return results in later steps
2. **Tool approval flows** where `tool-approval-response` should satisfy
the tool call requirement

## Summary

Modified `convertToLanguageModelPrompt` in
`packages/ai/src/prompt/convert-to-language-model-prompt.ts`:

- **Skip validation for provider-executed tools**: Tool calls with
`providerExecuted: true` are not required to have an immediate result,
as they have deferred results handled by the provider
- **Build approvalId → toolCallId mapping**: Extract the relationship
from `tool-approval-request` messages before conversion
- **Handle tool-approval-response**: When a `tool-approval-response` is
found, mark the corresponding tool call as satisfied using the mapping
- **Filter empty tool messages**: Ensure only valid, non-empty tool
messages are sent to the model

## Manual Verification

- Ran tests for provider-executed tools and tool approval flows
- Verified that `MissingToolResultError` is no longer thrown for valid
async/approval scenarios
- All tests in `packages/ai` pass

- [ ] Documentation has been added / updated (for bug fixes / features)
- [x] A _patch_ changeset for relevant packages has been added (for bug
fixes / features - run `pnpm changeset` in the project root)
- [x] I have reviewed this pull request (self-review)

# Releases
## @ai-sdk/amazon-bedrock@4.0.22

### Patch Changes

- 3318e0d: feat(provider/amazon-bedrock): add anthropic sub-provider
using native invokemodel api

Co-authored-by: vercel-ai-sdk[bot] <225926702+vercel-ai-sdk[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
feat(provider/amazon-bedrock): add anthropic sub-provider using native invokemodel api (#11878)

## background

this pr adds an `/anthropic` sub-module to `@ai-sdk/amazon-bedrock` that
bypasses the converse api entirely and uses the native anthropic
messages api through bedrock's invokemodel endpoint. this gives us full
feature parity with anthropic 1p api

## summary

- add `@ai-sdk/amazon-bedrock/anthropic` sub-module with
`bedrockAnthropic` and `createBedrockAnthropic`
- implement sigv4 authentication reusing existing
`createSigV4FetchFunction`
- implement bedrock event stream to sse transformer for streaming
support
- add anthropic tools support (computer use, text editor, bash)
- add 34 examples covering all functionality
- add unit tests for provider and fetch wrapper
- add e2e tests for stop_sequence functionality

## key implementation details

- uses `AnthropicMessagesLanguageModel` from
`@ai-sdk/anthropic/internal` (same pattern as google vertex anthropic)
- custom fetch wrapper transforms bedrock's
`application/vnd.amazon.eventstream` format to standard sse
- `transformRequestBody` handles bedrock-specific requirements:
- removes `stream` param (bedrock determines streaming by endpoint, not
body)
- removes `disable_parallel_tool_use` from `tool_choice` (bedrock
rejects it)
- maps tool versions to bedrock-supported versions (e.g.,
`bash_20241022` -> `bash_20250124`)
- maps tool names for newer versions (e.g., `str_replace_editor` ->
`str_replace_based_edit_tool` for `text_editor_20250728`)
- adds `anthropic_beta` to request body when computer use tools detected
  - adds `anthropic_version: 'bedrock-2023-05-31'`
- `supportedUrls: () => ({})` forces base64 for images (bedrock doesn't
support image urls)

## verification

tested against real bedrock api - all features working:

| feature | status |
|---------|--------|
| generateText | works |
| streamText | works |
| generateObject | works |
| streamObject | works |
| tools (custom with inputSchema) | works |
| computer use (bash, text_editor, computer) | works (claude 3.7+) |
| stop_sequence in streaming | works |
| image input (base64) | works |
| pdf input | works |
| multi-turn chat | works |
| cache_control | works |
| custom fetch | works |

- 34 example files created and tested

## refactoring

- extracted shared `createBedrockEventStreamDecoder` utility used by
both converse api and anthropic sub-provider
- uses `safeParseJSON` instead of direct `JSON.parse` per codebase
conventions

## feature parity with anthropic 1p

bedrock invokemodel has full feature parity with anthropic api, with
these exceptions (per anthropic docs):
- **files api** - not supported on bedrock or vertex
- **mcp connector** - not supported on bedrock or vertex
- **web_search** - tool calls are made but results return as
`error-json` (not supported on bedrock)
- **image urls** - bedrock only supports base64 (platform limitation,
not api)

## model compatibility

| feature | claude 3.5 | claude 3.7 | claude 4+ |
|---------|------------|------------|-----------|
| basic (text, object) | works | works | works |
| custom tools | works | works | works |
| computer use tools | not supported | works | works |
| reasoning | not supported | works | works |
| cache_control | works* | works | works |

*cache_control requires minimum 1024 tokens and preview access on some
models

supported claude 4 models on bedrock:
- `us.anthropic.claude-opus-4-5-20251101-v1:0`
- `us.anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929-v1:0`
- `us.anthropic.claude-opus-4-20250514-v1:0`
- `us.anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-20250514-v1:0`
- `us.anthropic.claude-opus-4-1-20250805-v1:0`
- `us.anthropic.claude-haiku-4-5-20251001-v1:0`

## future work

- create `@ai-sdk/anthropic-compatible` package as a reusable base for
anthropic-compatible providers
- refactor both bedrock anthropic and google vertex anthropic to use the
common anthropic-compatible package

## @ai-sdk/google-vertex@4.0.21

### Patch Changes

-   Updated dependencies [4729bed]
    -   @ai-sdk/anthropic@3.0.17

Co-authored-by: vercel-ai-sdk[bot] <225926702+vercel-ai-sdk[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
fix(anthropic): handle web_fetch error result JSON parsing gracefully (#11857)

## Background
Anthropic’s `web_fetch` tool can return typed error results (e.g.,
`web_fetch_tool_result_error`). These were causing a crash during prompt
serialization because `convertToAnthropicMessagesPrompt` attempted
`JSON.parse` on an object value, producing `"[object Object]" is not
valid JSON`.

## Summary
- Handle `web_fetch` error results when `output.value` is either a
string or an object, defaulting `error_code` to `'unknown'` on malformed
JSON.
- Added tests covering object-form and malformed-string error values.
- Added a patch changeset for `@ai-sdk/anthropic`.

## Manual Verification
- Ran package tests: `pnpm test:node` and `pnpm test:edge` in
`packages/anthropic`.
- Confirmed new web_fetch error-handling tests pass.

fix(openai-compatible): Accept non-OpenAI provider options (#11838)

## Background
```
providerOptions: {
  recraft: {
    style: 'vector_illustration',
  },
},
```
doesn't work, but
```
providerOptions: {
  openai: {
    style: 'vector_illustration',
  },
},
```
does, which is odd from a DX perspective

## Summary
Added support for other provider keys in the providerOptions object,
instead of hardcoding the check for OpenAI

## @ai-sdk/azure@3.0.13

### Patch Changes

-   Updated dependencies [000fa96]
    -   @ai-sdk/openai@3.0.13

## @ai-sdk/openai@3.0.13

### Patch Changes

- 000fa96: fix(openai): filter duplicate items when passing
conversationID

Co-authored-by: vercel-ai-sdk[bot] <225926702+vercel-ai-sdk[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
fix(openai): filter duplicate items when passing conversationID (#11899)

## Background

when using/passing conversation ID (for stateful messages) in the openai
provider, with tool calls, the provider sent the full conversation
history with every request. this clashed with the already present
message history provider via conversation ID.

which led to an error as seen in #11813 where duplicate item ids were
found

## Summary

introduce a flag `hasConversation` that allows us to skip re-sending
assistant messages that already exist in the conversation (identified by
having an item ID)

## Manual Verification

verified via the repro script in
`examples/ai-functions/src/generate-text/11813-repro.ts` (will be
removed after review) + tests added that simulate this scenario

Co-authored-by: vercel-ai-sdk[bot] <225926702+vercel-ai-sdk[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
fix(openai-compatible): use consistent camelCase for providerOptions key (#11842)

## Background

There's an inconsistency in the `@ai-sdk/openai-compatible` package
regarding the key used for provider options:

- **Top-level `providerOptions`** used kebab-case: `'openai-compatible'`
- **Per-message `providerOptions`** used camelCase: `openaiCompatible`

## Summary

- Use `openaiCompatible` (camelCase) as the primary providerOptions key
for consistency with per-message providerOptions
- The `openai-compatible` (kebab-case) key is deprecated but still
supported with a console warning
- Updated existing tests to use camelCase
- Added deprecation tests that verify the old key still works and emits
a warning

## Manual Verification

_tbd_

Co-authored-by: vercel-ai-sdk[bot] <225926702+vercel-ai-sdk[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
feat(ai): expose token usage in useChat onFinish callback (#11804)

## Background
In v5, `usage` (token counts) was available in `useChat`'s `onFinish`
callback via PR #2430. This was missing in v6, preventing users from
tracking token usage when chat responses complete. Developers who want
to track API costs or display token counts to users have no way to
access this data when a response completes.

## Summary
Added the `usage` field to the `onFinish` callback of `useChat`. Updated
corresponding tests as well.

Changes:
- Added usage field to the onFinish callback in useChat
- Propagated totalUsage from the finish chunk in streamText to the UI
message stream
- Added proper Zod schema (languageModelUsageSchema) for runtime
validation of usage data
- Updated TypeScript types to include usage?: LanguageModelUsage in
relevant interfaces

## Manual Verification
1. Ran the `next-openai` example with `pnpm dev`
2. Added an `onFinish` callback to `useChat` that logs `usage`
3. Sent a chat message and verified token counts appeared in the
console:
- `inputTokens`, `outputTokens`, `totalTokens` were all populated
correctly

## Checklist
- [x] Tests have been added / updated (for bug fixes / features)
- [x] Documentation has been added / updated (for bug fixes / features)
- [x] A _patch_ changeset for relevant packages has been added (for bug
fixes / features - run `pnpm changeset` in the project root)
- [x] I have reviewed this pull request (self-review)

## Background

visualizations and text-insights sourced from authoritative, real-time
data providers spanning finance, market research, sports, weather, and
politics. Instead of returning a list of links like a typical search
engine, Tako interprets your question semantically, retrieves trusted
data, and generates interactive, citable visual summaries (charts,
tables, text) that you can embed or integrate into apps, research tools,
or AI agents.: https://tako.com/

feat(provider/gateway): automate gateway model settings files update (#11656)


docs: add Codex App Server community provider (#11732)

## Summary

Adds documentation for the
[ai-sdk-provider-codex-app-server](https://github.com/pablof7z/ai-sdk-provider-codex-app-server)
community provider.

This provider uses Codex CLI's **app-server mode** (JSON-RPC over stdio)
rather than the one-shot `codex exec` mode, enabling:

- **Mid-execution message injection** - Send additional instructions
while the agent is actively working
- **Persistent threads** - Maintain conversation context across multiple
calls
- **Session control** - Interrupt running turns, inject messages at
checkpoints
- **Tool streaming** - Real-time visibility into command executions and
file changes

## Comparison with existing Codex CLI provider

| Feature | Codex CLI | Codex App Server |
|---------|-----------|------------------|
| Mid-execution inject | ❌ | ✅ |
| Persistent threads | ❌ | ✅ |
| Session control | ❌ | ✅ |
| Tool streaming | ❌ | ✅ |
| One-shot execution | ✅ | ✅ |

## Use Case

The existing Codex CLI provider is great for simple one-shot tasks. This
provider is for developers building human-in-the-loop workflows,
collaborative coding assistants, or applications that need real-time
course correction.

## Checklist

- [x] Provider published to GitHub
- [x] Documentation follows existing community provider format
- [x] Examples included

---------

Co-authored-by: Aayush Kapoor <83492835+aayush-kapoor@users.noreply.github.com>

the streaming phase, but the implementation only set

Co-authored-by: vercel-ai-sdk[bot] <225926702+vercel-ai-sdk[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
fix(ai): propagate providerMetadata during input-streaming state (#11805)

## Summary

Fixes providerMetadata not being propagated to tool invocations during
the `input-streaming` state.

## Problem

Provider-executed tools (like MCP tools) need to send metadata during
the streaming phase, but the current implementation only sets
`callProviderMetadata` when `part.state === "input-available"`. This
prevents providers from:

- Tracking tool hierarchy during streaming
- Sending provider-specific context before input is complete
- Using features that depend on early metadata availability

## Solution

- Remove the overly-restrictive state check in `processUIMessageStream`
(2 locations)
- Add `providerMetadata` to `tool-input-start` chunk schema and type
- Add `callProviderMetadata` to `input-streaming` state in validation
schemas
- Add `callProviderMetadata` to `UIToolInvocation` and
`DynamicToolUIPart` types

## Testing

- All 1740 existing tests pass
- Tested with provider-executed tools that send metadata during
streaming
- No breaking changes to existing behavior

## Related Issues

- #7694 (partially addresses metadata propagation aspect)
- #9163 (related to MCP tool support)

---------

Co-authored-by: More Studio <67614844+more-by-more@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: use `pnpm add <pkg>` instead of `pnpm install <pkg>` (#11171)

## Background

`pnpm install` is used to install all dependencies for a project while
`pnpm add <pkg>` is used to install a package and any packages that it
depends on.

## Summary

It should be `pnpm add <pkg>` instead of `pnpm install <pkg>`.

Co-authored-by: vercel-ai-sdk[bot] <225926702+vercel-ai-sdk[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
fix(mcp): lenient JSON-RPC parsing for http transport (#11801)

## Background

In AI SDK v6, we introduced MCP Client's inbuilt http transport - which
was very strict in how it handled responses that didn't conform to JSON
RPC spec.

Some servers would return non JSON-RPC format responses (like {}) for
notifications, which led to errors, as seen in #11792

## Summary

return early for notifications (messages without id) after successful
response

## Manual Verification

verified by running `examples/mcp/src/shopify-mcp/client.ts` before and
after the fix.

before -> threw the same error user saw
after -> proper streamed responsed from the model with the correct tools

## ai@6.0.37

### Patch Changes

- b5dab9b: fix(ai): maintain OpenTelemetry context across async
generator yields

Fixes an issue where OpenTelemetry context was lost at async generator
yield boundaries, causing nested ToolLoopAgent spans to escape to the
parent agent's level in observability platforms.

The fix ensures that when `recordSpan` is used with async generators
(e.g., in tool execution), the active context is explicitly maintained
using `context.with()`, preventing span hierarchy corruption in nested
agent scenarios.

    Closes #11720

fix(examples): move file to fix build breakage (#11809)

## Related Issues

Caused by #11766
fix(ai): maintain OpenTelemetry context across async generator yields (#11766)

## Background

When using nested `ToolLoopAgent` instances with telemetry enabled,
child agent spans were escaping to the parent agent's top level instead
of being correctly nested under the parent agent's tool call span. This
occurred because OpenTelemetry context was lost at async generator yield
boundaries when tools executed async generators (via `executeTool`).

This made it difficult to:

- Debug nested agent execution flows
- Accurately measure child agent performance
- Attribute costs correctly to child agents
- Understand the execution hierarchy in observability platforms like
Langfuse

## Summary

Modified `recordSpan` in `packages/ai/src/telemetry/record-span.ts` to
explicitly capture and maintain OpenTelemetry context using
`context.active()` and `context.with()`. This ensures that when async
generators yield (e.g., in tool execution), the active span context is
preserved across yield boundaries.

**Changes:**

- Added `context.active()` to capture the current context before
executing the function
- Wrapped function execution with `context.with()` to maintain context
across async generator yields
- Added comprehensive unit tests in
`packages/ai/src/telemetry/record-span.test.ts`
- Added integration test example in
`examples/ai-core/src/agent/openai-agent-nested-context.ts`
- Added changeset for patch release

## Manual Verification

Verified the fix using Vercel AI Gateway with nested `ToolLoopAgent`
instances:

1. **Unit Tests**: All 7 tests pass, verifying context propagation logic
2. **Integration Test**: Created nested agent scenario where:
  - Parent agent calls child agent via `call_child` tool
  - Child agent executes multiple steps with tool calls
- Verified all child agent spans correctly nest under `call_child` tool
call span
  - Verified no spans escape to parent agent's top level
3. **Multiple Loop Iterations**: Tested scenarios where both parent and
child agents loop multiple times, confirming all spans maintain correct
hierarchy

**Verification Results:**

- [x] All child agent spans correctly nested under parent tool call
- [x] No spans escaped to parent agent's top level
- [x] Multiple loop iterations work correctly
- [x] Span hierarchy matches expected structure

## Related Issues

Fixes #11720
feat(examples): OpenAI Resposes API previousResponseId using next.js and useChat (#11786)

## Background

OpenAI Responses API supports `previousResponseId` to let the
server-side replay conversation history without sending the full message
list every time.
However, the existing Next.js OpenAI examples don’t include an
end-to-end reference showing how to:

- keep the latest `responseId` on the client,
- pass it back to the server on the next request,
- send only the newest user message (and rely on OpenAI-side history
lookup),

This PR adds a minimal example demonstrating that pattern.

## Summary

- Added a new Next.js example route: `POST
/api/chat-openai-previous-response-id`

- Extracts `previousResponseId` from
`previousProviderMetadata.openai.responseId`
  - Calls `streamText()` with only the latest user message
- Passes `providerOptions.openai.previousResponseId` (with `store:
true`)
- Emits `data-providerMetadata` so the client can persist the latest
`responseId`
- Added a new example page: `/chat-openai-previous-response-id`

  - Stores the last `ProviderMetadata` in a `useRef`
- Sends `{ message: latestMessage, previousProviderMetadata }` per
request via `prepareSendMessagesRequest`
  - Updates the ref in `onData()` when `data-providerMetadata` arrives
  - Renders text + reasoning parts and a small tool UI example

## Manual Verification

`pnpm dev`
http://localhost:3000/chat-openai-previous-response-id

Co-authored-by: vercel-ai-sdk[bot] <225926702+vercel-ai-sdk[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
fix(provider-utils): improve tool type inference for inputExamples (#11777)

## Background

TypeScript was attempting to infer the generic `INPUT` type from both
`inputSchema` and `inputExamples`. Complex Zod schemas often produce
inferred types that are slightly different (e.g., stricter or
transformed) than literal objects, causing a conflict that resulted in
`never` or `any`.

## Solution

This PR addresses a TypeScript inference issue where `inputExamples`
would break type inference for the `execute` function's input parameter
when using Zod schemas with `.optional().default()` or `.refine()`.

Applied `NoInfer<INPUT>` to the `inputExamples` property in the `Tool`
type definition. This ensures that `INPUT` is established solely from
the `inputSchema`, which is the intended source of truth for the tool's
input structure.

### Changes
- Updated `packages/provider-utils/src/types/tool.ts` to use `NoInfer`
for `inputExamples`.
- Added regression tests in
`packages/provider-utils/src/types/tool.test-d.ts` covering the reported
scenarios.
- Included changeset for `@ai-sdk/provider-utils` and `ai`.

## Related Issues
Fixes #11673
docs: fix inputSchema Zod schema reference (#11807)

## Background

The existing `inputSchema` documentation linked to an outdated Zod
schema reference. The Zod documentation has since been reorganized, and
the previous link no longer points to the canonical Zod schema guide for
the AI SDK.

## Summary

Updated the `inputSchema` documentation to reference the correct Zod
schema documentation under `/docs/reference/ai-sdk-core/zod-schema`,
ensuring consistency with the current AI SDK docs structure and
preventing broken or misleading links.

Co-authored-by: vercel-ai-sdk[bot] <225926702+vercel-ai-sdk[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
fix(ai): throw error for missing start chunks in processUIMessageStream (#11463)

This PR addresses a runtime error: `TypeError: Cannot read properties of
undefined (reading 'text')` in `processUIMessageStream`.

### Issue
The issue occurs when `text-delta`, `reasoning-delta`, or
`tool-input-delta` chunks are received before their corresponding start
chunks (e.g., `text-start`). This can happen in malformed streams,
network issues causing packet reordering, or specific backend behaviors
where a delta is emitted immediately without a start frame.

Previously, `processUIMessageStream` assumed that a start part
(initialized in `activeTextParts`, etc.) always existed when a delta
arrived. When it didn't, accessing properties like `textPart.text`
caused a crash.

### Fix
This change adds explicit null checks for the active parts (`textPart`,
`reasoningPart`, `partialToolCall`). If a delta is received for a
missing part, it now throws a descriptive error:
> "Received text-delta for missing text part with ID "text-1". Ensure a
"text-start" chunk is sent before any "text-delta" chunks."

This descriptive error aids in debugging the actual upstream issue
(broken stream) rather than crashing the UI with an opaque TypeError.

## Changes
- **packages/ai/src/ui/process-ui-message-stream.ts**: Added guarding
`if (part == null)` checks in `text-delta`, `reasoning-delta`, and
`tool-input-delta` cases.
- **packages/ai/src/ui/process-ui-message-stream.test.ts**:
- Added test cases simulating malformed streams (delta without start)
for text, reasoning, and tool-inputs.
    - Verified that specific descriptive errors are thrown.
- Updated test harness `consumeStream` to properly propagate errors for
validation.

## Related Issues

Fixes #11700

docs: add `as const` to `Output.choice` example for proper typesafety (#11789)

## Background

The `Output.choice` example in the docs did not include an `as const`
which is useful for propert typesafety when using `Output.choice`.
Without the `as const` the `output` is `string[]` and with the `as
const` it infers that to be one of the options specified.

## Summary

Added `as const` at the end of the options array passed to
`Output.choice`

Fix OpenRouter docs' `streamText` example provided (#11367)

`streamText` returns an object with a property `textStream`, which is
the required iterable; the object itself is not iterable.

<!--
Welcome to contributing to AI SDK! We're excited to see your changes.

We suggest you read the following contributing guide we've created
before submitting:

https://github.com/vercel/ai/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
-->

## Background

<!-- Why was this change necessary? -->
When trying to run the example provided for streaming text:
<img width="872" height="518" alt="image"
src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/57abd1ea-bbfd-456e-bf16-146f71115280"
/>

An error was produced:

```ts
16 | for await (const chunk of result) {
                      ^
TypeError: undefined is not a function (near '...chunk of result...')
      at /workspaces/bloom/src/index.ts:16:18
```

This was due to the fact that the example provided in the docs assumes
that `streamText` returns a stream itself. This is incorrect; rather,
`streamText` returns an object with many properties, one of which is
`textStream`.

## Summary

<!-- What did you change? -->
Updated the example to use the correct destructured return property from
`streamText` (`textStream`)

## Manual Verification

<!--
For features & bugfixes.
Please explain how you *manually* verified that the change works
end-to-end as expected (excluding automated tests).
Remove the section if it's not needed (e.g. for docs).
-->
Ran the code on the latest version of `ai` with the `textStream`
property used. Verified it was now working as intended.

fix(ai): pass providerMetadata in smooth stream (#11688)

## Background

when using anthropic's extended thinking with tool calls, we drop the
thinking blocks from previous turns.

#11602

based on the reproduction provided, the issue turned out to be in
smoothStream, where smoothStream was stripping providerMetadata from
reasoning-delta chunks. this signature is required when sending thinking
blocks back to the API in subsequent turns

## Summary

pass `providerMetadata` through smoothStream

## Manual Verification

the reproduction code [given
here](https://gist.github.com/p3droml/503c5d4888182a75222377109bfdd291)
was run before and after the fix, and was resolved

Co-authored-by: vercel-ai-sdk[bot] <225926702+vercel-ai-sdk[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
fix(compat, groq): send reasoning-end before text-start in streaming (#11751)

## Background

similar to what was fixed in #11675, we were emitting the
`reasoning-end` blocks at stream end, rather than when content blocks
actually finished.

Before:
```
reasoning-start → reasoning-delta → ... → text-start → text-delta → ... → reasoning-end (at flush) → text-end
```

After:
```
reasoning-start → reasoning-delta → ... → reasoning-end → text-start → text-delta → ... → text-end
```

#11683

## Summary

`reasoning-end` is emitted before transitioning to text or tool calls,
not deferred until stream flush

## Manual Verification

n/a - asked the user if they noticed it for a specific

## Background

Added additional configuration parameters for the Perplexity Search tool
in the AI Gateway provider to provide more control over search results.

## Summary

Enhanced the Perplexity Search tool with additional parameters:
- Added `maxTokens` parameter to control the maximum total tokens across
all search results
- Added `lastUpdatedAfterFilter` and `lastUpdatedBeforeFilter`
parameters to filter results by update date
- Updated documentation with parameter descriptions and default values
- Clarified that `maxTokensPerPage` default is 2048 (was previously
documented as 1024)

## Manual Verification

API and that the documentation accurately reflects the available
options.

fix(provider-utils): add additionalProperty field for standard schema function (#11748)

## Background

when using structured outputs with arktype schema validation, we weren't
applying the `additionalProperties` field to the JSON obejct - which
then led to invalid outputs.

we applied it for zod schema but not standard schema.

#11705

## Summary

wrap the function `standardSchema()` with
`addAdditionalPropertiesToJsonSchema()` which ensures
`additionalProperties: false` is added recursively to all object types

## Manual Verification

verified by running example
`examples/ai-core/src/generate-text/openai-output-object-arktype.ts`
which fails before we apply the fix

# Releases
## @ai-sdk/xai@3.0.18

### Patch Changes

-   9ed771c: feat(provider/xai): add image support to responses api

Co-authored-by: vercel-ai-sdk[bot] <225926702+vercel-ai-sdk[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
feat(provider/xai): add image support to responses api (#11736)

## Background

xai responses api supports images via `input_image` content type but we
weren't implementing it - just emitting a warning and dropping the
image. this caused users to get responses like "i am unable to view
images" when using `xai.responses()` with vision models.

## Summary

- add `XaiResponsesUserMessageContentPart` type with `input_text` and
`input_image`
- convert image file parts to `input_image` format (url or base64)
- handle `image/*` wildcard media type (defaults to `image/jpeg`)
- throw `UnsupportedFunctionalityError` for non-image file types (pdf,
etc)
- add example files for generate-text and stream-text

## Verification

tested with live xai api - both url and base64 images work correctly
with `grok-2-vision-1212`.

## Checklist

- [x] Tests have been added / updated (for bug fixes / features)
- [x] Documentation has been added / updated (for bug fixes / features)
- [x] A _patch_ changeset for relevant packages has been added (for bug
fixes / features - run `pnpm changeset` in the project root)
- [x] I have reviewed this pull request (self-review)

---------

Co-authored-by: Walter Korman <shaper@vercel.com>
docs(elevenlabs): update voice argument usage and examples in ElevenLabs provider documentation (#11742)

The current documentation at
[`content/providers/01-ai-sdk-providers/90-elevenlabs.mdx`](content/providers/01-ai-sdk-providers/90-elevenlabs.mdx)
shows an example using `generateSpeech` but doesn't demonstrate the
`voice` argument. The `voice` parameter is a direct argument to
`generateSpeech` (not a provider option) and accepts a voice ID from
ElevenLabs.

This pull request modifies the example code block to include the `voice`
argument:

```ts
import { experimental_generateSpeech as generateSpeech } from 'ai';
import { elevenlabs } from '@ai-sdk/elevenlabs';

const result = await generateSpeech({
  model: elevenlabs.speech('eleven_multilingual_v2'),
  text: 'Hello, world!',
  voice: '21m00Tcm4TlvDq8ikWAM', // ElevenLabs voice ID
});
```

## FYI

- The default voice ID is `21m00Tcm4TlvDq8ikWAM` (Rachel) per [the
provider
implementation](https://github.com/vercel/ai/blob/8a6317d6404e600187e5191bba0ef7e8733addd4/packages/elevenlabs/src/elevenlabs-speech-model.ts#L71)
- Voice IDs can be found on the ElevenLabs website in the Voice Library
- The `voice` parameter is optional; when omitted, it uses the default
voice

## Future Work

- Rachel is a Legacy voice scheduled for deprecation on February 28,
2026 and will be replaced by the "Janet" voice
([source](https://help.elevenlabs.io/hc/en-us/articles/26928417254801-What-are-Legacy-voices#:~:text=Legacy%20voices%20are%20former%20Default,all%20products%20after%20that%20date.))
- Review documentation for othe providers that use the `generateSpeech`
API to make sure they document the `voice` argument

Co-authored-by: vercel-ai-sdk[bot] <225926702+vercel-ai-sdk[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
fix(anthropic): add application/pdf type regex matching (#11690)

## Background

Reported in #11685, the `supportedUrls` config was missing regex
matching for application/pdf type, which in turn led to the SDK
downloading the PDF's first.

This wasn't needed for anthropic provider since it directly supported
reading pdfs from URL.

But before this fix, if the connection while downloading the pdf was a
little flaky, it would lead to a socket timeout error.

## Summary

add the regex url pattern for `application/pdf` type

## Manual Verification

manual verification was a little erratic since running the repro code
given by the user only sometimes gave the error.
but there was still a feature gap

## Checklist

- [x] Tests have been added / updated (for bug fixes / features)
- [ ] Documentation has been added / updated (for bug fixes / features)
- [x] A _patch_ changeset for relevant packages has been added (for bug
fixes / features - run `pnpm changeset` in the project root)
- [x] I have reviewed this pull request (self-review)

## Related Issues

Fixes #11685

fix(anthropic): read real input_tokens in message_delta block (#11611)

## Background

The Anthropic SDK only read `input_tokens` from `message_start` block
now, but a lot of compatible providers only return real tokens in
`message_delta` block.

## Summary

Compare existing input_tokens with those in the message_delta block(and,
existing in message_delta). If they do not match, use the data from
message_delta.

## Manual Verification

It will change nothing in official provider.
